Button

Stable

Versatile button component with multiple variants, sizes, and states. Supports loading, icons, and full-width layouts.

Overview

The Button component is the primary action trigger in the design system. It supports multiple visual variants, three sizes, loading state, and full-width mode. Every button is built with accessibility as a first-class concern — focus-visible rings, proper ARIA attributes, and keyboard navigation are built in.

Live Demo

Click the buttons below to see the component in action. The loading button simulates a 2-second async operation.

Variants

Sizes

States

The loading button simulates a 2-second async operation. Click it to see the spinner in action.

Variants

Buttons come in four visual variants to match different levels of emphasis and purpose.

button-variants.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function ButtonVariants() {
  return (
    <div className="flex gap-3">
      <Button variant="primary">Primary</Button>
      <Button variant="secondary">Secondary</Button>
      <Button variant="ghost">Ghost</Button>
      <Button variant="danger">Danger</Button>
    </div>
  );
}

Sizes

Three sizes cover most use cases: small for tight spaces, medium as the default, and large for primary CTAs.

button-sizes.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function ButtonSizes() {
  return (
    <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
      <Button size="sm">Small</Button>
      <Button size="md">Medium</Button>
      <Button size="lg">Large</Button>
    </div>
  );
}

Loading State

The isLoading prop shows a spinner and disables interaction, which is critical for async actions like form submissions or API calls to prevent duplicate operations.

button-loading.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function ButtonLoading() {
  return (
    <Button isLoading>
      Saving...
    </Button>
  );
}

Full Width

Use fullWidth for buttons that should span their container — ideal for mobile layouts and form submissions.

button-fullwidth.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function ButtonFullWidth() {
  return (
    <Button fullWidth>
      Submit
    </Button>
  );
}

Disabled State

button-disabled.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function ButtonDisabled() {
  return (
    <Button disabled>
      Cannot Click
    </Button>
  );
}

Props

Prop Type Default Description
variant "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost" | "danger" "primary" Visual style variant for different emphasis levels.
size "sm" | "md" | "lg" "md" Button size. Use sm for compact UIs, lg for primary CTAs.
isLoading boolean false Shows a spinner and disables interaction during async operations.
fullWidth boolean false Makes the button span 100% of its parent container width.
disabled boolean false Disables the button, reducing opacity and preventing interaction.
className string - Additional CSS classes to merge with default styles (uses tailwind-merge).
children React.ReactNode - Content to render inside the button. Can include icons or text.

Edge Cases & Error States

Icon-Only Buttons (No Children Text)

When using Button with only an icon and no visible children, always provide an aria-label so screen readers can convey the action. The button maintains proper focus-visible ring styling even without text content.

button-icon-only.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function IconOnlyButton() {
  return (
    <Button variant="ghost" aria-label="Delete item">
      <svg className="h-5 w-5" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth={2}>
        <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" d="M19 7l-.867 12.142A2 2 0 0116.138 21H7.862a2 2 0 01-1.995-1.858L5 7m5 4v6m4-6v6m1-10V4a1 1 0 00-1-1h-4a1 1 0 00-1 1v3M4 7h16" />
      </svg>
    </Button>
  );
}

Long Button Text

Buttons do not truncate text. Long labels will wrap to multiple lines inside the button. If you need a fixed-width button, set a className with whitespace-nowrap to prevent wrapping.

button-long-text.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function LongTextButton() {
  return (
    <>
      {/* Wraps naturally */}
      <Button variant="secondary">Save all changes and continue editing later</Button>

      {/* Force single line */}
      <Button className="whitespace-nowrap">Save all changes</Button>
    </>
  );
}

FullWidth Inside Flex Containers

When a fullWidth button is inside a flex container with flex-wrap, it will stretch to fill the parent width. This works as expected for mobile layouts but can cause unexpected stretching in complex grid or flex arrangements.

button-fullwidth-flex.tsx 
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function FullWidthInFlex() {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
      <Button fullWidth>This spans the entire flex row</Button>
      <Button>Compact</Button>
    </div>
  );
}

Loading + Disabled Interaction

When isLoading is true, the button is automatically disabled regardless of the disabled prop value. This prevents double-submission during async operations even if both props are accidentally passed.

Best Practices & Usage Guidelines

Variant Selection

  • Primary — The main call-to-action. Use at most one per view or form.
  • Secondary — For alternative actions alongside a primary button. Pair with primary in dialogs and modals.
  • Ghost — For subtle actions like "Cancel" or "Edit". Good in card headers and toolbars.
  • Danger — For destructive actions (delete, remove, archive). Never use for primary CTAs.

Form Integration

  • Always set type="submit" when Button is inside a form
  • Use isLoading during form submission to prevent double-submission
  • Place the primary action button on the left, secondary on the right in dialog footers
  • Use fullWidth on mobile viewports; constrain width with max-w-xs on desktop

Accessibility

  • Button uses a native <button> element — keyboard navigation (Enter/Space) is built-in
  • The focus-visible ring only shows for keyboard users, not mouse clicks
  • When using icon-only buttons, always provide aria-label
  • Loading state disables the button automatically so screen readers announce it as disabled
  • All color variants maintain WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 for text)

Implementation Details

Component Architecture

Button uses React.forwardRef to pass the ref to the underlying native <button> element, enabling parent components to focus the button programmatically or use it with tooltip/popper libraries. displayName is set for React DevTools debugging.

ClassName Merging

The component uses cn() (a tailwind-merge + clsx wrapper from @storyhouse/utils) to merge the consumer's className prop with internal styles. This resolves Tailwind class conflicts automatically — if you pass className="bg-red-500", it overrides the default variant background while preserving all other computed classes.

Dark Mode

Button uses Tailwind dark mode variants (dark: prefix) on color classes. This means all four variants automatically adapt in dark mode without any prop changes or JS logic. Dark mode is driven by the class strategy on the <html> element.

Loading State Architecture

When isLoading is enabled, an animated spinner element is rendered before children. The spinner uses animate-spin with a Tailwind border trick (transparent top border). All animations respect the prefers-reduced-motion media query via the globals.css override.

Accessibility

  • Uses native <button> element for correct keyboard semantics
  • Focus-visible ring styles ensure keyboard users see focus indicators
  • Disabled state reduces opacity and removes pointer events
  • Loading state prevents double-submission of forms
  • Spinner animation respects prefers-reduced-motion
  • Button text is clearly readable for all color variants (high contrast ratio)

Interactive Preview
